topics.explanation: A budget helps you plan income and expenses so you can control spending.
Saving means setting aside part of your income for future needs.
examples: Saving ₹5,000 every month creates ₹60,000 in a year.
tips: Pay yourself first by saving before spending.
mistakes: Saving only what is left at the end of the month.

topics.explanation: Managing loans and credit card balances effectively.
examples: Paying off a high-interest credit card before a low-interest loan.
tips: Always pay at least the minimum amount due.
mistakes: Taking new loans to pay old loans.

topics.explanation: A credit score reflects your creditworthiness.
examples: Paying bills on time improves your score.
mistakes: Missing EMI or credit card payments.

topics.explanation: A fixed deposit offers a fixed interest rate for a specific period.
examples: Invest ₹50,000 in an FD for 1 year.
mistakes: Breaking an FD early and paying penalties.

topics.explanation: Interest earned on both the principal and previous interest.
examples: ₹10,000 invested at 8% grows faster over time.

topics.explanation: EMI is the fixed monthly payment for a loan.
examples: A ₹5 lakh loan may require a monthly EMI payment.
tips: Choose an EMI that fits your budget.
mistakes: Borrowing more than you can repay.

topics.explanation: Insurance protects you financially from unexpected risks.
Inflation is the rise in prices over time.
examples: A product costing ₹100 today may cost ₹110 next year.
tips: Invest in assets that can outpace inflation.

topics.explanation: Saving money for specific goals such as education, travel, or a house.
